title: cuphea ingrata f. parvifolia
intro: Cuphea ingrata f. parvifolia, also known as Mexican heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small, white, and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Cuphea ingrata f. parvifolia include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tea, and it can also be used in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions.
